page: Amazon Web Services r8a.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server includes 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong computational and memory capabilities. The server achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as stress-ng single-core CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks register in the top 25%, while Passmark database operations and bzip3 decompression benchmarks reach the top 10%. For language model workloads, the instance processes small 135M models and medium 7B models with top-tier and strong token generation speeds, respectively. However, Passmark memory score benchmarks place the system in the average category.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8a.8xlarge offers high resource density with its DDR5 memory and dedicated AMD EPYC cores, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of local storage. The lack of bundled local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, limiting its utility for local-storage-heavy databases or large-scale GPU-accelerated deep learning. This profile is best suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, software compilation, and text or image processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place the server in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, software compilation, and Passmark database operations, while memory bandwidth tests fall in the top 25%. Conversely, the Passmark memory score is in the middle 50%. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference, offering a high-density memory configuration without the added cost of local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 15 Gbps network baseline, delivering top-tier database and CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
256.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in database operations and software compilation benchmarks
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for memory-intensive applications, database workloads, and text processing
